questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Roads in India are built and maintained by the Central Government.
questions.reason: Reason (R): The Central Government is responsible for the construction and maintenance of national highways.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This is correct because national highways are a part of the Central Government's infrastructure development projects.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The Indian Railways network is one of the largest in the world.
questions.reason: Reason (R): It is owned and operated by the Central Government.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This is correct because the Indian Railways network is indeed one of the largest in the world, and it is owned and operated by the Central Government.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The Central Government is responsible for the development of ports in India.

(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This is incorrect because while ports are crucial for international trade and commerce, their development is a shared responsibility between the Central and State Governments.
